title: knema stellata subsp. minahassae
intro: Knema stellata subsp. minahassae, also known as Minahassa nut, is a plant species in the family Myristicaceae, native to Sulawesi in Indonesia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in the treatment of various health conditions due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Minahassa nut include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to support immune system function. Traditionally, the plant is prepared by extracting the oil from its seeds, which can be used in herbal preparations such as capsules, tinctures, and infusions.
